jobs.title: Team Member
jobs.description: Team Member is our front-of-house and back-of-house position covering bartender, barback, cashier, cook, prep cook, dishwasher, and busser/food runner duties. You'll be cross-trained and may rotate between roles based on your strengths and the day's needs. All Team Members share in the tip pool.
responsibilities: Provide warm, efficient service to every guest
Prepare food and beverages to El Pueblo standards
Handle cash and card transactions accurately
Maintain a clean, organized work area
Follow all food safety and health code requirements
Work collaboratively across stations as the shift requires
requirements: Reliable, positive attitude, and a strong work ethic
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
Physical stamina to stand for full shifts and lift up to 25 lbs
Flexible schedule including evenings, weekends, and holidays
Food Handler card (or willing to obtain within 30 days)
Bartender role requires alcohol service certification (RBS)
benefits: One free meal during your shift

jobs.title: Shift Lead
jobs.description: Shift Leads run the floor during their shift — coaching Team Members, handling guest issues, opening and closing the restaurant, and acting as the manager-on-duty when GMs are off. Shift Leads share in the tip pool.
responsibilities: Lead and support Team Members on every shift
Open and close the restaurant according to checklist
Resolve guest concerns with hospitality and discretion
Track inventory and flag low stock to management
Ensure food safety and cleanliness standards are met
Train new hires on station procedures
requirements: 1+ year of restaurant leadership or supervisory experience
Food Handler card (RBS for any alcohol service)
Flexible schedule including evenings, weekends, and holidays
benefits: One free meal during your shift

jobs.title: Assistant Manager
jobs.description: Assistant Managers partner with the General Manager to run a single El Pueblo location — staffing, scheduling, P&L, guest experience, vendor relationships, and team development. Assistant Managers are accountable for the restaurant when the GM is off.
responsibilities: Co-own the restaurant's operations, financials, and culture
Build schedules and manage labor cost
Hire, train, coach, and (when needed) discipline staff
Drive guest satisfaction and respond to escalated issues
Step in for the General Manager on their off days
requirements: Demonstrated ability to lead a team of 15+
Comfortable with P&L, labor reports, and scheduling software
Food Handler + Manager certification (RBS for alcohol)
Flexible schedule including evenings, weekends, and holidays
benefits: One free meal during your shift
